lede: A look at November 19 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Evgenia Medvedeva and Jessicah Schipper, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2023 Cricket World Cup final
events.desc: The 2023 Cricket World Cup final takes place at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad, India, played between host nation India and Australia.

events.title: 2013 Iranian embassy bombing in Beirut
events.desc: A double suicide bombing at the Iranian embassy in Beirut kills 23 people and injures 160 others.

events.title: Pike River Mine disaster
events.desc: The first of four explosions takes place at the Pike River Mine in New Zealand. Twenty-nine people are killed in the nation's worst mining disaster since 1914.

events.title: Space Shuttle program
events.desc: Space Shuttle program: Columbia is launched on STS-80, which would become the longest mission in the program at 17 days. On this mission, astronaut Story Musgrave becomes the only astronaut to fly on all five space shuttles.

events.title: Iran hostage crisis
events.desc: Iran hostage crisis: Iranian leader Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini orders the release of 13 female and black American hostages being held at the US Embassy in Tehran.

events.title: Afghanistan
events.desc: Afghanistan, Iceland and Sweden join the United Nations.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Battle between HMAS Sydney and HSK Kormoran. The two ships sink each other off the coast of Western Australia, with the loss of 645 Australians and about 77 German seamen.

events.title: Doom Bar
events.desc: The Doom Bar in Cornwall claims two ships, Island Maid and Angele, the latter killing the entire crew except the captain.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: U.S. President Abraham Lincoln delivers the Gettysburg Address at the dedication ceremony for the military cemetery at Gettysburg, Pennsylvania.

events.title: Jay Treaty
events.desc: The United States and the Kingdom of Great Britain sign Jay's Treaty, which attempts to resolve some of the lingering problems left over from the American Revolutionary War.

births.name: Réjean Lemelin
births.line: Canadian ice hockey player and coach

births.name: Indira Gandhi
births.line: Indian politician, 3rd Prime Minister of India (died 1984)

deaths.text: Trina Schart Hyman, American author and illustrator
Rudolf Kinau, German writer in Low German
William Nelson (governor), American politician, Colonial Governor of Virginia

observances.text: Christian feast day: Severinus, Exuperius, and Felician
Day of Discovery of Puerto Rico (Puerto Rico)
Day of Missile Forces and Artillery (Russia, Belarus)
